nativeRange.habitat: Shallow coastal reef zones and lagoons; typically found on coral rubble, sandy substrate, and hard substrate in the photic zone

summary: The Pom Pom (Gracilaria hayi) is a bushy red macroalgae prized for its compact, spherical clumps and fast growth. It is commonly kept both as an ornamental display specimen and as a nutrient-exporting refugium alga, and is generally considered one of the more approachable macroalgae for reef hobbyists.
careNotes:
Pom Pom (Gracilaria hayi) is a distinctive red macroalgae that forms tight, rounded, bushy clumps — a shape that gives the species its trade name. It is widely kept for two purposes: as an ornamental display specimen and as a nutrient-exporting alga that readily absorbs nitrates, phosphates, and dissolved organic compounds from the water column. The species is also noted for its resistance to biofouling, meaning it tends not to accumulate unwanted growth on its fronds under typical reef conditions.

The Pom Pom does well in standard reef water parameters: temperature 22–26°C, pH 8.1–8.4, salinity SG 1.023–1.025, and alkalinity 8–12 dKH. Lighting is generally considered moderate — a standard 5000K reef fixture with an 8-hour photoperiod tends to suffice. Supplementation with magnesium, trace elements, and iron is optional but has been reported to support growth.

Placement options include a refugium sump, where the alga can be gently tumbled with moderate flow, or direct display in the main tank. The species is generally considered reef-safe with corals and invertebrates, but most herbivorous fish — tangs and rabbitfish in particular — will readily consume it. In tanks with dedicated herbivores, it is often used intentionally as a food crop rather than as a display plant.

Several related Gracilaria species appear in the trade alongside Pom Pom, including Thick Ogo (Gracilaria sp.), Red Ogo (G. parvispora), Green Ogo (G. tikvahiae), Red Spaghetti (G. pacifica), and Monkey Finger (G. salicornia). Propagation is primarily by fragmentation; trimmed sections can be replanted or fed to herbivorous tankmates.

IMPORTANT — ATTACHMENT: As a red alga, Pom Pom will MELT at the base if buried in sand. It should be attached to rockwork instead, using superglue gel, zip-tie, fishing line, or by wedging into a crevice. Sand-bed placement is generally suitable only for green algae such as Caulerpa, Halimeda, Penicillus, Rhipocephalus, and Udotea, and some calcified Dasycladales.

additional.name: Mosaic Macros
additional.notes: Confirmed species as Gracilaria hayi under trade name 'Red Bush Gracilaria'; minimal husbandry parameters. Sells as 2–3" plug and 5"+ cuttings.

additional.name: Top Shelf Aquatics
additional.notes: Confirmed nutrient export and display use; noted herbivore palatability. Minimal parameter data.

additional.name: Mosaic Macros general macroalgae care guide
additional.notes: Slice-wide secondary source — provides authoritative Mosaic-wide parameter targets (temp 22-26°C, pH 8.1-8.4, Ca 400-450 ppm, Mg 1250-1350 ppm, NO3 5-20 ppm, PO4 0.02-0.1 ppm) and the 4-method propagation standard. Added retroactively 2026-06-08.
